evals.prompt: I just got a CSV export of our customer orders — about 200k rows, 30 columns — and I've never seen this data before. We eventually want a churn model out of it. Where do I start?
evals.expected_output: Should run a structured EDA workflow: profile columns first, examine missingness patterns and distributions, look at the target definition and class balance, flag potential leakage columns, and turn findings into explicit hypotheses — not jump to modeling.
assertions: Profiles the dataset first (column types, ranges, cardinality, summary statistics) before giving any modeling advice
Examines missingness patterns and distinguishes random gaps from structural ones (e.g., a column only populated for some segment)
Checks distributions and flags outliers or skew with concrete methods (quantiles, histograms, IQR or similar)
Warns about target leakage — columns that would not be available at prediction time must be identified and excluded
Examines how the churn target would be defined and its class balance before any modeling
Looks at relationships between candidate features and the target, not just univariate profiles
Produces explicit hypotheses or follow-up questions from the findings rather than only reporting statistics

evals.prompt: I need to understand a warehouse extract with 400 columns and 3 million rows by Friday, and pandas kills my laptop when I load it. What's the plan?
evals.expected_output: Should scale the EDA workflow instead of abandoning it: triage columns cheaply first, work on a representative sample or push aggregations to the warehouse, and prioritize by the analysis goal rather than exhaustively profiling everything.
assertions: Recommends sampling or chunked/out-of-core processing instead of loading everything into memory
Notes the sample must be representative (random or stratified) and flags the caveat that rare events and outliers can be missed under sampling
Triages the 400 columns cheaply (types, null rates, cardinality) before deep-diving any of them
Prioritizes columns by relevance to the stated goal rather than profiling all 400 equally
Suggests pushing heavy aggregations down to the warehouse via SQL, or using a lazy/out-of-core engine (e.g., Polars, DuckDB, Dask)
Still covers the core EDA checklist (types, missingness, distributions, key relationships) on the reduced view

evals.prompt: Our finance dashboard has shown wrong revenue numbers all week — the nightly job loads orders into the warehouse and something is off upstream. Can you help me analyze the data and find the problem?
evals.expected_output: Should recognize this as a pipeline-level data-trust problem, not exploration of an unfamiliar dataset — hand off to the data-quality skill (dbt tests, freshness, schema drift, contracts, lineage) rather than starting a dataset-profiling workflow.
assertions: Recognizes this as a data-quality / pipeline-trust problem rather than exploratory analysis of a new dataset
Hands off to or invokes the data-quality skill instead of running a generic EDA workflow
Points at concrete data-quality directions (dbt tests, source freshness, schema drift, upstream contract, lineage/blast radius) as the right path
Does not respond with a dataset-profiling/distributions workflow as the primary answer
